title: elymus dahuricus
intro: Elymus dahuricus, also known as Dahurian wildrye, is a perennial grass native to East Asia and the Russian Far East. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Elymus dahuricus include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Elymus dahuricus can be used as a decoction or tea, or dried and made into a powder for capsule or tincture form.
